name: Life Cube
description: Life Cube is a rapid deployment shelter system that is transported in a 5 x 5′ container, opened onto a platform, and then inflated.

taxonomy.sub_category: Life Cube Updated on March 4, 2024 · Created on September 10, 2017 Life Cube Upcoming Update Life Cube is a rapid deployment shelter system that is transported in a 5 x 5′ container, opened onto a platform, and then inflated. Developed By Unknown Tested By Life Cube Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Upcoming Update Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Life Cube is a rapid deployment shelter system that is transported in a 5 x 5′ container, opened onto a platform, and then inflated. It sets up in 5 minutes. The interior is aluminum for rigidity and has hardened floors for any terrain. It has integrated solar power, batteries, radio and lights. Target Users (Target Impact Group) Distributors / Implementing Organizations Life Cube Inc. Manufacturing/Building Method Units are produced to order. The 5 x 5' shipping container transforms into the floor of the tent to offer a hard, raised platform from the ground. It is made of ABS plastic and can be airdropped from an airplane, or placed on a standard trailer or bed of a pickup truck. Intellectural Property Type Select Type User Provision Model Users can purchase directly from Life Cube or organizations can obtain the tents from Life Cube to distribute in post-disaster and refugee camp situations. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Intended number of occupants (#) 5 occupants Speed of assembly (h) 5 min (0.08 h) Footprint area (m²) 10.75 m 2 (116 sq ft) Internal volume (m³) ~40 m³ Material composition Container: ABS plastic , Canopy: urethane-coated nylon Lifespan (years) Unknown Flammable flash point temperature (ºC) The canopy and beam material is made of fire retardant thermoplastic material . However, the flammable flash point temperature is unknown. Maximum snow load (kg/m²) Unknown Maximum wind speed (km/h) When fully deployed and anchored: 80.4 km/h. During canopy inflation/deployment: 130 km/h Suitable climates Temperate Design Specifications The Life Cube comes with the following components : Shipping Container/Hard Surface Raised Floor Inflatable Canopy Two Position Table (Optional) Power Module (Optional) Tie Down Kit The material is made from 1000 denier air-retention fabrics that are laser cut, and all seams are RF welded. Before shipping, all units undergo full pressure inflation testing. The canopy is made out of urethane-coated nylon that is waterproof and MSHA-rated fire retardant. Full specifications can be found in the user manual . Technical Support Instructions for how to assemble and disassemble the Life Cube can be found in the user manual . Each tent has a unique serial number to track the product's warranty and provide customer service. Users can submit a help ticket on the Life Cube website for support. Replacement Components Life Cube offers instructions for how to patch the fabric and a one year limited warranty on all components. Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Manufacturer specified performance targets include durability, off-grid functionality, portability, and programmatic flexibility. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety Fabric is waterproof and fire retardant. Complementary Technical Systems The Life Cube has an integrated power system that is powered by solar panels on the roof. Academic Research and References None Compliance with regulations Unknown Other Information None Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
